title: Case created

title: Case created
body:
Orbitel Communications
Network Service Incident Report — Repeated Call Drop & Data Outage
Ticket ID:
Date Reported:
Circle / Region:
Customer Name:
Account / Mobile No.:
Plan:
Status:
Summary of Issue
ORB-2026-334190
July 10, 2026
Chennai — Tamil Nadu
Ananya Krishnan
XXXXXX8823
Postpaid Unlimited 5G — 599
Escalated to Network Operations
Since July 5, 2026, the customer has experienced repeated call drops during outgoing and incoming
voice calls, along with intermittent loss of mobile data connectivity in the Velachery and Adyar areas of
Chennai. The issue occurs primarily between 6:00 PM and 10:00 PM on weekdays. The customer
reports an average of 6-8 dropped calls per day and data speeds falling below 1 Mbps despite an
active 5G plan. A prior ticket (ORB-2026-331004) was closed without resolution on July 6.
Reported Incidents
Date
Time
Issue Type
Location
Jul 5
Jul 6
19:12
20:45
Call Drop
Data Outage
Duration
Velachery
3 calls
Adyar
Jul 8
Jul 9
18:30
21:05
Call Drop
No Signal
Velachery
47 min
5 calls
Velachery
Jul 10
19:50
Customer Statement
Data Outage
Adyar
22 min
1 hr 10 min
“I depend on my phone for work calls in the evening and this has been going on for almost a week now.
Calls disconnect mid-conversation and data barely loads a webpage. I raised a ticket earlier but it was
closed with no explanation and the problem is still there.”
Network Operations Notes
Diagnostics indicate elevated cell congestion on tower ORB-CHN-0119 (Velachery sector) during peak
evening hours, with utilization consistently above 92 percent capacity. A secondary fault was identified
on the fiber backhaul link serving the Adyar micro-cell cluster, causing intermittent data-session drops.
Field engineering has scheduled a capacity upgrade for the Velachery tower and a backhaul link
replacement for Adyar, both targeted for completion within 5 business days.
Resolution Plan
1. Temporary load-balancing applied to Velachery sector to reduce congestion, effective immediately.
2. Backhaul fiber replacement at Adyar micro-cell scheduled for July 14, 2026.
3. Customer to receive SMS confirmation once both fixes are deployed.
4. One-time service credit of 250 to be applied to the customer's next bill for the disruption.
5. Follow-up call scheduled for July 16, 2026 to confirm resolution
